title: Modifiable blockchain transaction
abstract: A computer-implemented method for generating a non-final blockchain transaction comprising a first portion is provided. The non-final blockchain transaction comprises is for signing by a signer, and for modifying after signing to generate a modifiable blockchain transaction. A first locking script is provided, which is configured, when executed together with a first unlocking script of a signature blockchain transaction, to reconstruct the non-final blockchain transaction based on the modifiable blockchain transaction. The non-final blockchain transaction is available to the signer.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS 1. A computer-implemented method for generating a non-final blockchain transaction comprising a first portion, wherein the non-final blockchain transaction is for signing by a signer, wherein the non-final blockchain transaction is for modifying after signing to generate a modifiable blockchain transaction, the method comprising: providing a first locking script, wherein the first locking script is configured, when executed together with a first unlocking script of a signature blockchain transaction, to reconstruct the non-final blockchain transaction based on the modifiable blockchain transaction; and making the non-final blockchain transaction available to the signer. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein the method further comprises: determining that a signature has been generated based on the non-final blockchain transaction; and in response to determining that the signature has been generated, modifying the non-final blockchain transaction to include a second portion to generate the modifiable blockchain transaction; and making the modifiable blockchain transaction available to one or more nodes of a blockchain network. 3. A computer-implemented method for generating a modifiable blockchain transaction comprising a first portion and a second portion, wherein a non-final blockchain transaction is a blockchain transaction comprising the first portion and not the second portion, the method comprising: obtaining the non-final blockchain transaction, wherein the non-final blockchain transaction comprises a first locking script which is configured, when executed together with a first unlocking script of a signature blockchain transaction, to reconstruct the non- final blockchain transaction based on the modifiable blockchain transaction; determining that a signature has been generated based on the non-final blockchain transaction; and 

 in response to determining that the signature has been generated, modifying the non-final blockchain transaction to include the second portion to generate the modifiable blockchain transaction; and making the modifiable blockchain transaction available to one or more nodes of a blockchain network. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein the method further comprises: generating the non-final blockchain transaction by providing the first locking script, thereby obtaining the non-final blockchain transaction; and making the non-final blockchain transaction available to a signer for generating the signature. 5. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the first locking script is further configured, when executed together with a first unlocking script of a signature blockchain transaction, to verify the signature provided in the first unlocking script based on the non- final blockchain transaction. 6. The method of claim 5, wherein the first locking script is further configured to: generate a non-final signature hash preimage derived from the non-final blockchain transaction, wherein the non-final signature hash preimage is a message signed by the signature; wherein the signature is verified if it is derivable from the generated non-final signature hash preimage. 7. The method of any preceding claim, wherein the first locking script is further configured to: obtain a copy of the modifiable blockchain transaction; wherein the non-final blockchain transaction is reconstructed by removing, from the copy of the modifiable blockchain transaction, the second portion to reconstruct the non- final blockchain transaction. 8. The method of claim 7, wherein the modifiable blockchain transaction comprises one or more unlocking scripts, wherein the non-final blockchain transaction is reconstructed by further removing, from the modifiable blockchain transaction, the one or more unlocking scripts. 9. The method of claim 7 or claim 8, wherein the first locking script is configured to obtain the copy of the modifiable blockchain transaction by extracting, from the first unlocking script a serialised copy of the modifiable blockchain transaction, wherein the serialised copy of the modifiable blockchain transaction is the copy of the modifiable blockchain transaction. 10. The method of any of claims 6 to 9, wherein the non-final signature hash preimage is generated by: hashing the non-final blockchain transaction to compute a transaction identifier of the non- final blockchain transaction; and providing the transaction identifier of the non-final blockchain transaction in a transaction identifier field of the non-final signature hash preimage. 11. The method of claim 10, wherein the first locking script is further configured to: obtain a copy of a signature hash preimage corresponding to the modifiable blockchain transaction; wherein the non-final signature hash preimage is generated by replacing a hash field of the signature hash preimage of the modifiable blockchain transaction with the transaction identifier of the non-final blockchain transaction. 12. The method of claim 11, wherein the non-final signature hash preimage is generated by further: modifying a script code field of the signature hash preimage of the modifiable blockchain transaction to remove a script segment associated with the second portion to generate a non-final script code field. 13. Computer equipment comprising: 

 memory comprising one or more memory units; and processing apparatus comprising one or more processing units, wherein the memory stores code arranged to run on the processing apparatus, the code being configured so as when on the processing apparatus to perform the method of any of claims 1 to 12. 14. A computer program embodied on computer-readable storage and configured so as, when run on one or more processors, to perform the method of any of claims 1 to 12. 15. A blockchain-based data structure, comprising: a modifiable blockchain transaction comprising an instruction reconstruct a non-final transaction, and a signature blockchain transaction comprising: a reference to the first transaction; and data used to reconstruct the non-final transaction.
